title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for rewarding foul-baiting and flopping, impacting game outcomes.
Brown was ejected in a game against the Spurs, expressing dissatisfaction with officiating decisions publicly.
In a loss to Oklahoma City, Brown scored 34 points and played a game-high 41 minutes for the Celtics.

title: Oscars 2026 predictions: Full list of who will win each category
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "One Battle After Another" is predicted to win Best Picture at the 2026 Oscars, with "Sinners" as a close contender.
Michael B. Jordan is expected to win Best Actor for "Sinners," although Ethan Hawke is favored by the article's author.
Paul Thomas Anderson is predicted to secure Best Director for "One Battle After Another" after winning multiple precursor awards.

title: Why new Patriots safety Kevin Byard predicted New England’s breakthrough season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard signed a one-year, $9 million contract with the New England Patriots to reunite with coach Mike Vrabel.
Byard, who led the NFL with seven interceptions last season, aims to strengthen the Patriots' secondary alongside key players.
Byard believes coach Mike Vrabel's leadership will guide the Patriots to a potential Super Bowl run in the upcoming season.

title: ‘Our small way of saying thank you’: A mother-son duo is running the Boston Marathon for MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lisa and Benjamin Levine from Needham will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH).
The Levines' participation honors MGH's role in saving family members' lives during serious health crises.
MGH has significantly contributed to their family through patient care and management of life-threatening food allergies.

title: $25M winning lottery ticket sold at Turners Falls convenience store
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A $25M winning lottery ticket was sold at Cumberland Farms on Montague City Road, Turners Falls.
Trustee Renat Lumpau accepted a one-time payment of $16.25M before taxes on behalf of Open Door Trust.
The store received a $50,000 bonus for selling the ticket; two grand prizes remain in the game.

title: After prior uproar over removal, new mayor to restore Italian flag road lines to Newton
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Newton Mayor Marc Laredo announced the restoration of Italian flag road lines on Adams Street in Nonantum.
The red, white, and green stripes were removed last summer for safety and regulatory compliance by former Mayor Ruthanne Fuller.
Designated parking lanes now permit the tricolor road markings, following changes by Newton's Department of Public Works.

title: Student charged in Danvers murder allegedly told police he ‘wanted to kill someone for a long time’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion in the death of 68-year-old Janet Swallow in Danvers.
Police found DeMayo carrying a knife on Standish Street, and he allegedly confessed to the murder during interrogation.
Judge ordered DeMayo to undergo a psychiatric evaluation to assess competence before court proceedings.

title: Revolution announce return of original homegrown player Diego Fagundez on 1-year contract
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Diego Fagundez has signed a one-year contract with New England Revolution, returning to his first professional club.
Fagundez, an MLS veteran, previously played for Austin FC and LA Galaxy, winning the 2024 MLS Cup.
The Revolution, currently winless, hopes Fagundez's experience boosts their attack under new coach Marko Mitrović.

title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Parts of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will close from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday.
Closure affects North Harvard Street to Mugar Way for maintenance of the Storrow Drive tunnel.
Drivers will be detoured via Eliot Bridge, Memorial Drive, and Longfellow Bridge amid potential rain delays.

title: South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston officials will enforce strict measures against public drinking during the St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
Police warn paradegoers about drink spiking, advising vigilance and the use of drink-testing tools to prevent incidents.
City officials and agencies aim to prevent past parade issues like violence, disorder, and public assaults through a new task force.
